title: Hieracium schisticolor
taxonomy: Hieracium schisticolor is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a member of the genus Hieracium, which consists of hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is further divided into several subgenera and sections based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. Hieracium schisticolor is typically classified as a member of the subgenus Pilosella. Within the subgenus Pilosella, it falls under the section Hieracium sect. Pilosella.

intro: Hieracium schisticolor, also known as meadow hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion-like genus Hieracium, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and jagged, lobed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Bioactive constituents of Hieracium schisticolor include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to support digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
